name: 2017 EULAR/ACR Classification Criteria for Adult and Juvenile Idiopathic Inflammatory Myopathies
citation: Lundberg IE, et al. Ann Rheum Dis. 2017;76(12):1955-1964.
classification_rule: Probability-based score. Probability ≥55% = possible IIM; ≥60% definite with biopsy; ≥90% definite without biopsy.

items_without_biopsy.label: Age of onset of first symptom related to disease ≥18 and <40 yrs
Age of onset of first symptom ≥40 yrs
Objective symmetric weakness, proximal upper extremities
Objective symmetric weakness, proximal lower extremities
Neck flexors relatively weaker than neck extensors
In legs, proximal muscles relatively weaker than distal
Elevated serum CK or LDH or AST or ALT

items_with_biopsy_addon.label: Endomysial infiltrate without invading non-necrotic muscle fibres

notes_on_4_7_reasoning: For Asian male with multi-organ inflammation, normal CK but muscle soreness and interstitial lung findings, strongly weight anti-MDA5 dermatomyositis.
Anti-MDA5, anti-Jo-1, anti-PL-7/PL-12, anti-TIF1γ sit on the MYOP18 panel — pending results are rate-limiting.
TIF1γ positivity in adults ≥40 raises cancer screen urgency (paraneoplastic DM).
